questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Unit Self-Assessment Program (USAP)?
options: To replace external inspection teams entirely
To accurately identify and report issues to the command chain so Airmen feel they can safely report what is true
To conduct annual performance evaluations of unit personnel
To document individual disciplinary actions for the commander

questions.question: What tool is a USAF program of record used for managing Unit Self-Assessment Programs (USAPs)?
options: Inspector General Evaluation Management System (IGEMS)
Self-Assessment Communicator (SAC)
Management Internal Control Toolset (MICT)
Automated Inspection Tracking System (AITS)

questions.question: Under the Air Force Complaints Resolution Program, complaints may be submitted through all of the following EXCEPT:
options: In person or by phone
Through electronic means or in writing
Anonymously through social media without identification
To supervisors, first sergeants, commanders, or members of Congress

questions.question: Who is the only authority that may investigate allegations of reprisal and restriction under the Military Whistleblower's Protection Act?
options: The member's immediate commander
The Staff Judge Advocate
The Inspector General (IG)
The first sergeant

questions.question: According to AFI 31-218, at what blood alcohol concentration (BAC) is a person considered impaired on a military installation, even without other evidence?
options: Greater than .05
Greater than .06
Greater than .08
Greater than .10

questions.question: What is the length of the driving privilege suspension for driving under the influence at a BAC of 0.08 percent or greater on a military installation?
options: Six months
One year
Two years
Permanent revocation

questions.question: Why should commander-directed drug testing be used only as a last resort?
options: It requires approval from the Secretary of the Air Force
The results may not be used for disciplinary action under the UCMJ or to characterize an administrative discharge
It can only be administered during duty hours
It requires a search and seizure authorization from a military judge

questions.question: Under the self-identification provision for substance abuse, what protection is afforded to a member who voluntarily seeks help?
options: The member is guaranteed retention in the USAF
The member receives automatic promotion protection
Commanders may not use the self-identification against the member in UCMJ action or when characterizing an administrative discharge
The member is exempt from all future drug testing

questions.question: What is fraternization, as defined in the UCMJ?
options: Any social interaction between officers and enlisted members
An unprofessional relationship between an officer and enlisted member prohibited by Article 134, UCMJ
A romantic relationship between any two members of different ranks
An unprofessional relationship between any military member and a civilian employee

questions.question: When fraternization occurs between an officer and an enlisted member, who is held primarily responsible and subject to disciplinary action for fraternization under Article 134, UCMJ?
options: Both are equally held responsible under Article 134
The enlisted member
The officer
The member with the longer time in service

questions.question: What are the six distinct forms of harassment covered by the Air Force Equal Opportunity Program?
options: Verbal, physical, emotional, cyber, institutional, and environmental
Discriminatory, sexual, bullying, hazing, retaliation, and reprisal
Racial, gender, religious, disability, age, and genetic
Direct, indirect, quid pro quo, hostile environment, systemic, and retaliatory

questions.question: Within what timeframe must military formal Equal Opportunity complaints be filed?
options: 30 calendar days of the alleged offense
45 calendar days of the alleged offense
60 calendar days of the alleged offense
90 calendar days of the alleged offense

questions.question: Who may file military Equal Opportunity complaints?
options: Only active duty military personnel
Military personnel, their family members, and retirees
Any person affiliated with the Department of Defense
Military personnel and civilian employees of the USAF

questions.question: What is the Unfavorable Information File (UIF) used for?
options: To store all personnel records for a member's entire career
To provide commanders with an official and single means of filing derogatory data about a member's conduct and duty performance
To document only court-martial convictions for the member's record
To record positive accomplishments that offset disciplinary actions

questions.question: How long is the initial observation period when a commander places an individual on the control roster?
options: Three months
Six months
Nine months
Twelve months

questions.question: How many duty days does an individual have to acknowledge and provide pertinent information before the commander makes a final decision on a UIF action?
options: One duty day
Three duty days
Five duty days
Seven duty days

questions.question: What standard of proof is used in administrative action proceedings to evaluate whether misconduct occurred?
options: Beyond a reasonable doubt
Clear and convincing evidence
Preponderance of the evidence
Probable cause

questions.question: What is the purpose of the control roster?
options: To permanently mark a member's record with a disciplinary action
To serve as a substitute for nonjudicial punishment
To establish an observation period as a rehabilitative tool for individuals who fail to meet standards
To initiate involuntary separation proceedings

questions.question: Which administrative action indicates a stronger degree of official censure than a letter of counseling and a letter of admonishment?
options: Control roster placement
Letter of reprimand
Record of individual counseling
Unfavorable Information File entry

questions.question: What are the three possible service characterizations for members administratively discharged under DAFI 36-3211?
options: Honorable, general (under honorable conditions), and bad conduct
Honorable, general (under honorable conditions), and under other than honorable conditions
Honorable, dishonorable, and bad conduct
Honorable, general, and dishonorable

questions.question: Within how many years of the date of separation may a former USAF member request review by the Air Force Discharge Review Board?
options: 5 years
10 years
15 years
20 years

questions.question: What is the highest level of administrative review for correcting military records?
options: Air Force Discharge Review Board
Air Force Board for Correction of Military Records
Secretary of the Air Force Personnel Council
Inspector General Appeals Board

questions.question: Which of the following is true about the two sexual assault reporting options in the USAF?
options: A victim may convert an unrestricted report to a restricted report at any time
A victim may convert a restricted report to an unrestricted report at any time
Both restricted and unrestricted reports trigger an immediate investigation
Only officers may elect the restricted reporting option

questions.question: According to the chapter, retaliation is prohibited by which article of the UCMJ?
options: Article 92
Article 120
Article 132
Article 134

questions.question: When a member is not rehabilitated within six months of being placed on the control roster, what action must the commander take?
options: Grant an automatic six-month extension
Remove the member from the control roster with no further action
Initiate more severe action
Refer the case to the Inspector General

questions.question: What are Self-Assessment Communicators (SACs) designed to do?
options: Replace the Inspector General inspection process entirely
Provide individual performance feedback to supervisors
Improve compliance with published guidance and communicate risk and program health up and down the chain of command
Document safety violations for OSHA compliance

questions.question: According to AFH 1, what are the four Major Graded Areas (MGAs) in the Air Force Inspection System?
options: Training, personnel, logistics, and finance
Safety, security, readiness, and compliance
Administration, operations, maintenance, and communications
Managing resources, leading people, improving the unit, and executing the mission

questions.question: According to AFH 1, what is the primary objective of the Air Force Inspection System (AFIS)?
options: To foster a culture of critical self-assessment and continuous improvement and reduce reliance on external inspection teams
To punish units that fail to meet standards
To replace the chain of command for enforcement purposes
To conduct quarterly evaluations of all USAF squadrons

questions.question: Under the Military Whistleblower's Protection Act, what is prohibited?
options: Filing anonymous complaints with the Inspector General
Restricting or retaliating against a member for making a lawful communication to Congress or an Inspector General
Discussing complaints with legal counsel before filing
Submitting complaints through the chain of command

questions.question: What is the driving privilege suspension for a second DUI/DWI offense on a military installation?
options: One year
Two years
Three years
Permanent revocation

questions.question: What are the four types of drug testing authorized for use in the USAF?
options: Random, probable cause, commander-directed, and voluntary
Annual, quarterly, incident-based, and random
Inspection (unit sweep), probable cause (search and seizure authorization), commander-directed, and consent
Entrance, periodic, random, and involuntary

questions.question: According to AFH 1, unprofessional relationships are those that do NOT respect which differences?
options: Age, education, and experience differences
Cultural, ethnic, and religious differences
Gender, race, and national origin differences
Differences in grade, rank, or position

questions.question: What are the two types of sexual harassment defined in AFH 1?
options: Quid pro quo and hostile work environment
Direct and indirect harassment
Verbal and physical harassment
Individual and systemic harassment

questions.question: Under restricted reporting for sexual assault, who can a victim disclose the assault to while maintaining confidentiality?
options: Their immediate supervisor and first sergeant only
The Inspector General and Staff Judge Advocate
Any military member of equal or higher rank
A Sexual Assault Response Coordinator (SARC), victim advocate, or healthcare provider

questions.question: What is a Letter of Counseling (LOC) used for?
options: A formal disciplinary tool that is permanently filed in the member's records
An administrative tool to document minor infractions or to clarify expected standards of behavior
A document indicating the member is on the control roster
A legal document that initiates UCMJ proceedings

questions.question: What is the correct order of administrative actions from least to most severe?
options: Letter of reprimand, letter of admonishment, letter of counseling
Letter of counseling, letter of reprimand, letter of admonishment
Letter of counseling, letter of admonishment, letter of reprimand
Letter of admonishment, letter of counseling, letter of reprimand

questions.question: According to AFH 1, nonjudicial punishment under Article 15, UCMJ, is available to commanders to deal with what?
options: Only felony-level criminal offenses
Minor acts of indiscipline
Only offenses committed during deployment
Civilian misconduct on military installations

questions.question: What is the Air Force Board for Correction of Military Records (AFBCMR)?
options: A board that reviews fitness assessment failures
A panel that determines promotion eligibility
A board that conducts criminal investigations
The highest level of administrative review for correcting military records

questions.question: According to AFH 1, retaliation under Article 132, UCMJ, includes which of the following acts?
options: Only physical harm against the reporting individual
Filing a counter-complaint through the Inspector General
Ostracism and acts of maltreatment or cruelty against the reporting individual
Reassigning the reporting individual to a different unit

questions.question: How many duty days does an individual have to respond with a written statement before the commander makes a final decision on an Unfavorable Information File (UIF) action?
options: One duty day
Three duty days
Five duty days
Seven duty days
